name: Find the Biggest Number
description: Given a list of integers `nums`, find and return the largest (maximum) number in the list.

name: Two Sum
description:
Find a pair of indices `i` and `j` in an integer list `nums` such that `nums[i] + nums[j]` equals `target`, with the condition that `i != j`.

Each input is guaranteed to have one unique solution. Return the indices as a pair `[i, j]` with the smaller index first.
constraints: *Exactly one solution exists for each testcase.*

name: Contains Duplicate
description:
Given an list of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices `i` and `j` such as that `nums[i] + nums[j] == target` and `i != j`.

You may assume that *every* input has exactly 1 pair of indices `i` and `j` that satisfy this condition.

Return the answer with the smaller index first.

name: Valid Anagram
description: Determine if string `t` is a permutation of string `s` (an anagram), meaning it contains the exact same characters with the same frequencies, just possibly in a different order. Return `True` if it is, and `False` otherwise.
constraints: `s` and `t` consist of lowercase English letters.

name: Design Dynamic List (Resizable List)
description:
Design a resizable list class named `DynamicList` to manage a list of integers dynamically.

The class should implement the following functionality:

* `DynamicList(capacity)` initializes the list with a starting capacity of `capacity` (always greater than zero).
* `get(i)` retrieves the value at index `i`.
* `set(i, val)` replaces the value at index `i` with `val`.
* `pushback(val)` appends `val` to the end of the list, automatically doubling the capacity via `resize()` if the list is full.
* `popback()` removes and returns the last element of the list.
* `resize()` doubles the current capacity of the list, copying all existing elements over.
* `getSize()` returns the current number of elements.
* `getCapacity()` returns the total capacity of the list.
constraints: At most `5000` method calls in total.

name: Max Consecutive Ones
description: Find the length of the longest contiguous sublist consisting only of the number `1` within a binary list `nums` containing only `0`s and `1`s.

name: Longest Common Prefix
description: Find the longest common prefix string amongst an list of strings `strs`. If there is no common prefix, return an empty string.
constraints: `strs[i]` consists of only lowercase English letters.

name: Replace Elements With Greatest Element On Right Side
description: Given an list `arr`, replace each element with the largest element among the elements to its right, and replace the last element with `-1`. Return the modified list.

name: Remove Element
description: Given an integer list `nums` and an integer `val`, remove all occurrences of `val` in-place. The order of the elements may be changed. Return the number of elements in `nums` which are not equal to `val`.

name: Number of Students Unable to Eat Lunch
description: You are given two integer lists `students` and `sandwiches` where `sandwiches[i]` is the type of the sandwich in the stack and `students[j]` is the preference of the student in the queue. Return the number of students unable to eat.

name: Majority Element
description: Given an list `nums` of size `n`, return the majority element. The majority element is the element that appears more than `⌊n / 2⌋` times. You may assume that the majority element always exists in the list.

name: Repeated Substring Pattern
description: Given a string `s`, check if it can be constructed by taking a substring of it and repeating multiple times.

name: Range Sum Query - Immutable
description:
Design a class `NumList` that supports calculating the sum of a prefix range of elements in an integer list.

The class should implement the following functionality:

* `NumList(nums)` initializes the object with the integer list `nums`.
* `sumRange(left, right)` returns the sum of the elements of `nums` between indices `left` and `right` inclusive (i.e. `nums[left] + nums[left + 1] + ... + nums[right]`).
constraints: At most `10^4` calls will be made to `sumRange`.

name: Design HashSet
description:
Design a HashSet without using any built-in hash table libraries.

Implement `MyHashSet` class:

* `add(key)` Inserts the value `key` into the HashSet.
* `contains(key)` Returns whether the value `key` exists in the HashSet or not.
* `remove(key)` Removes the value `key` in the HashSet. If `key` does not exist in the HashSet, do nothing.
constraints: At most `10^4` calls will be made to `add`, `remove`, and `contains`.

examples.output: [null, null, null, true, false, null, true, null, false]

name: Design HashMap
description:
Design a HashMap without using any built-in hash table libraries.

Implement the `MyHashMap` class:

* `put(key, value)` inserts a `(key, value)` pair into the HashMap. If the `key` already exists in the map, update the corresponding `value`.
* `get(key)` returns the `value` to which the specified `key` is mapped, or `-1` if this map contains no mapping for the `key`.
* `remove(key)` removes the mapping for the `key` if this map contains the mapping for the `key`.
constraints: At most `10^4` calls will be made to `put`, `get`, and `remove`.

name: Group Anagrams
description: Given an list of strings `strs`, group the anagrams together. To ensure deterministic testing, return the groups sorted lexicographically, and the strings within each group sorted lexicographically.

name: Top K Frequent Elements
description: Given an integer list `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k` most frequent elements. To make the output deterministic, return the final list of elements sorted in ascending order.

name: Encode and Decode Strings
description: Design an algorithm to encode a list of strings to a single string, and decode the single string back to the original list of strings. Implement `Codec` class with `encode` and `decode` methods.
constraints: `strs[i]` consists of any printable ASCII characters.

name: Range Sum Query 2D - Immutable
description:
Design a 2D matrix class `NumMatrix` that supports calculating the sum of a submatrix region.

The class should implement the following functionality:

* `NumMatrix(matrix)` initializes the object with the 2D integer matrix `matrix`.
* `sumRegion(row1, col1, row2, col2)` returns the sum of the elements of `matrix` inside the rectangle defined by its upper left corner `(row1, col1)` and lower right corner `(row2, col2)` inclusive.
constraints: At most `10^4` calls will be made to `sumRegion`.

name: Product of List Except Self
description: Given an integer list `nums`, return an list `answer` such that `answer[i]` is equal to the product of all the elements of `nums` except `nums[i]`. The product is guaranteed to fit in a 32-bit integer. You must write an algorithm that runs in $O(N)$ time and without using the division operation.

name: Valid Sudoku
description:
Determine if a $9 \times 9$ Sudoku board is valid. Only the filled cells need to be validated according to the following rules:

* Each row must contain the digits `1-9` without repetition.
* Each column must contain the digits `1-9` without repetition.
* Each of the nine $3 \times 3$ sub-boxes of the grid must contain the digits `1-9` without repetition.

Note: A Sudoku board (partially filled) could be valid but is not necessarily solvable. Only the filled cells need to be validated.

name: Longest Consecutive Sequence
description: Given an unsorted list of integers `nums`, return the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence. You must write an algorithm that runs in $O(N)$ time.

name: Best Time to Buy And Sell Stock II
description: You are given an integer list `prices` where `prices[i]` is the price of a given stock on the $i$-th day. On each day, you may decide to buy and/or sell the stock. You can only hold at most one share of the stock at any time. Find and return the maximum profit you can achieve.

name: Majority Element II
description: Given an integer list `nums` of size $n$, find all elements that appear more than $\lfloor n / 3 \rfloor$ times. To ensure deterministic testing, return the final list of elements sorted in ascending order.

name: Repeated DNA Sequences
description: Given a string `s` that represents a DNA sequence, return all the `10`-letter-long sequences (substrings) that occur more than once in a DNA molecule. To ensure deterministic testing, return the final list of substrings sorted lexicographically.

name: Sublist Sum Equals K
description: Given an list of integers `nums` and an integer `k`, return the total number of sublists whose sum equals to `k`.

name: First Missing Positive
description: Given an unsorted integer list `nums`, return the smallest positive integer that is not present in `nums`. You must implement an algorithm that runs in $O(N)$ time and uses $O(1)$ auxiliary space.

name: Design Bogo Sort
description: Implement Bogo Sort to sort an integer list `nums` in ascending order. Bogo Sort (permutation sort) successively generates permutations of its input until it finds one that is sorted. *Note: due to $O(N \times N!)$ complexity, the list sizes are very small.*

name: Design Bubble Sort
description: Implement Bubble Sort to sort an integer list `nums` in ascending order. Bubble Sort works by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.

name: Design Selection Sort
description: Implement Selection Sort to sort an integer list `nums` in ascending order. Selection Sort works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted part and putting it at the beginning.

name: Design Insertion Sort
description: Implement Insertion Sort to sort an integer list `nums` in ascending order. Insertion Sort works by building the sorted list one item at a time, inserting each element into its proper position relative to the already sorted part.

name: Design Merge Sort
description: Implement Merge Sort to sort an integer list `nums` in ascending order. Merge Sort is a divide-and-conquer algorithm that recursively splits the list in half, sorts each half, and merges the sorted halves.

name: Design Quick Sort
description: Implement Quick Sort to sort an integer list `nums` in ascending order. Quick Sort is a divide-and-conquer algorithm that selects a 'pivot' element, partitions the list such that elements less than pivot go left and elements greater than pivot go right, and then recursively sorts the sub-lists.

name: Design Counting Sort
description: Implement Counting Sort to sort an integer list `nums` in ascending order. Counting Sort works by counting the number of occurrences of each unique value in the list, and using arithmetic to calculate their target positions in the sorted output. *Note: Counting Sort is efficient for lists where the range of elements ($K$) is small.*

name: Design Bucket Sort
description: Implement Bucket Sort to sort an integer list `nums` in ascending order. Bucket Sort divides elements into several 'buckets', sorts each bucket individually (e.g. using insertion sort or another sorting algorithm), and then concatenates the buckets. *Note: Bucket Sort assumes the inputs are uniformly distributed over a range.*

name: Design Radix Sort
description: Implement Radix Sort to sort an integer list `nums` in ascending order. Radix Sort is a non-comparative sorting algorithm that sorts integers by grouping keys by individual digits that share the same significant position and value. *Note: Radix Sort is efficient for sorting non-negative integers.*

name: Reverse String
description: Write a function that reverses a list of characters `s`. You must return the reversed list.

name: Valid Palindrome
description: Given a string `s`, return `true` if it is a palindrome, or `false` otherwise. A palindrome is a string that reads the same backward as forward after converting all uppercase letters into lowercase letters and removing all non-alphanumeric characters.
constraints: `s` consists only of printable ASCII characters.

examples.input: s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"

name: Valid Palindrome II
description: Given a string `s`, return `true` if the `s` can be a palindrome after deleting **at most one** character from it.

name: Merge Strings Alternately
description: You are given two strings `word1` and `word2`. Merge the strings by adding letters in alternating order, starting with `word1`. If a string is longer than the other, append the additional letters onto the end of the merged string.
constraints: `word1` and `word2` consist of lowercase English letters.

name: Merge Sorted List
code: merge(nums1: list[int], m: int, nums2: list[int], n: int) -> list[int]
description: You are given two integer lists `nums1` and `nums2`, sorted in non-decreasing order, and two integers `m` and `n`, representing the number of elements in `nums1` and `nums2` respectively. `nums1` has a length of `m + n`, where the first `m` elements denote the elements that should be merged, and the last `n` elements are set to `0` and should be ignored. Merge `nums1` and `nums2` into a single list sorted in non-decreasing order and return the merged list.

name: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List
description: Given an integer list `nums` sorted in non-decreasing order, remove the duplicates in-place such that each unique element appears only once. Return the number of unique elements `k` in `nums`.

name: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List II
description: Remove duplicates in-place from a sorted integer list `nums` such that each unique element is allowed to appear at most two times. Return the number of elements `k` in the modified list.

name: Two Sum II - Input List Is Sorted
description: Given a **1-indexed** list of integers `numbers` that is already sorted in non-decreasing order, find two numbers such that they add up to a specific `target` number. Return the indices of the two numbers, `[index1, index2]`, added by one. Your solution must use only constant extra space.

name: 3Sum
description: Given an integer list `nums`, return all the unique triplets `[nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]]` such that `i != j`, `i != k`, `j != k`, and `nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0`. Return the triplets sorted.

name: 4Sum
description: Given an list `nums` of `n` integers and an integer `target`, return an list of all the **unique** quadruplets `[nums[a], nums[b], nums[c], nums[d]]` such that their sum equals `target`. Return the quadruplets sorted.

name: Rotate List
description: Given an integer list `nums`, rotate the list to the right by `k` steps, where `k` is non-negative. Return the rotated list.

name: Container With Most Water
description: You are given an integer list `height` of length `n`. Find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container, such that the container contains the most water. Return the maximum amount of water a container can store.

name: Boats to Save People
description: You are given an list `people` representing weights, and a weight limit for each boat. Each boat carries at most two people at the same time, provided their combined weight is at most `limit`. Return the minimum number of boats to carry every person.

name: Trapping Rain Water
description: Given `n` non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is `1`, compute how much water it can trap after raining.

name: Contains Duplicate II
description: Given an integer list `nums` and an integer `k`, return `True` if there are two distinct indices `i` and `j` in the list such that `nums[i] == nums[j]` and `abs(i - j) <= k`.

name: Best Time to Buy And Sell Stock
description: Given an list `prices` where `prices[i]` is the price of a given stock on the `i`-th day, return the maximum profit you can achieve from this transaction. If you cannot achieve any profit, return `0`.

name: Number of Sub Lists of Size K and Avg >= Threshold
code: numOfSublists(arr: list[int], k: int, threshold: int) -> int
description: Given an list of integers `arr` and two integers `k` and `threshold`, return the number of sub-lists of size `k` whose average is greater than or equal to `threshold`.

name: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string `s`, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
constraints: `s` consists of English letters, digits, symbols, and spaces.

name: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
description: You are given a string `s` consisting of uppercase English letters. You can choose any character of the string and change it to any other uppercase English character. You can perform this operation at most `k` times. Return the length of the longest substring containing the same letter you can get after performing the above operations.
constraints: `s` consists of only uppercase English letters.

name: Permutation In String
description: Given two strings `s1` and `s2`, return `True` if `s2` contains a permutation of `s1`, or `False` otherwise.
constraints: `s1` and `s2` consist of lowercase English letters.

name: Minimum Size Sublist Sum
description: Given an list of positive integers `nums` and a positive integer `target`, return the minimal length of a contiguous sublist of which the sum is greater than or equal to `target`. If there is no such sublist, return `0` instead.

name: Find K Closest Elements
code: findClosestElements(arr: list[int], k: int, x: int) -> list[int]
description: Given a sorted integer list `arr`, two integers `k` and `x`, return the `k` closest integers to `x` in the list. The result should also be sorted in ascending order.

name: Design Hit Counter
description: Design a hit counter which counts the number of hits received in the past 300 seconds (i.e., the time range `[timestamp - 300 + 1, timestamp]`).
constraints: All timestamps are in non-decreasing order.
At most `10^4` calls will be made to `hit` and `getHits`.

name: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given two strings `s` and `t` of lengths `m` and `n` respectively, return the minimum window substring of `s` such that every character in `t` (including duplicates) is included in the window. If there is no such substring, return the empty string ``.
constraints: `s` and `t` consist of uppercase and lowercase English letters.

name: Sliding Window Maximum
description: You are given an list of integers `nums`, there is a sliding window of size `k` which is moving from the very left of the list to the very right. You can only see the `k` numbers in the window. Each time the sliding window moves right by one position. Return the max sliding window.

name: Binary Search
description: Given an list of integers `nums` which is sorted in ascending order, and an integer `target`, write a function to search `target` in `nums`. If `target` exists, then return its index. Otherwise, return `-1`.
constraints: All the integers in `nums` are unique.

name: Search Insert Position
description: Given a sorted list of distinct integers `nums` and a target value `target`, return the index if the target is found. If not, return the index where it would be if it were inserted in order.
constraints: `nums` contains distinct values sorted in ascending order.

name: Guess Number Higher Or Lower
description: We are playing the Guess Game. The game is as follows: I choose a number from `1` to `n`. You have to guess which number I chose. Every time you guess wrong, I will tell you whether the number I chose is higher or lower than your guess. You call a pre-defined API `guess(num: int) -> int` which returns: `-1` if your guess is higher than the pick, `1` if your guess is lower than the pick, and `0` if your guess is equal to the pick.

name: Sqrt(x)
description: Given a non-negative integer `x`, return the square root of `x` rounded down to the nearest integer. The returned integer should be non-negative as well. You must not use any built-in exponent function or operator.

name: Search a 2D Matrix
description: You are given an `m x n` integer matrix `matrix` with the following two properties: Each row is sorted in non-decreasing order. The first integer of each row is greater than the last integer of the previous row. Given an integer `target`, return `True` if `target` is in `matrix` or `False` otherwise.

name: Koko Eating Bananas
description: Return the minimum integer `k` such that Koko can eat all the bananas from all piles within `h` hours.

name: Capacity to Ship Packages Within D Days
description: Return the least weight capacity of the ship that will result in all the packages on the conveyor belt being shipped within `days` days.

name: Find Minimum In Rotated Sorted List
description: Given a sorted rotated list `nums` of unique elements, return the minimum element of this list. You must write an algorithm that runs in $O(\log n)$ time.
constraints: All the integers of `nums` are unique.

name: Search In Rotated Sorted List
description: Given the list `nums` after a possible rotation and an integer `target`, return the index of `target` if it is in `nums`, or `-1` if it is not in `nums`.

name: Search In Rotated Sorted List II
description: Given the list `nums` after a possible rotation containing duplicates, and an integer `target`, return `True` if `target` is in `nums`, or `False` otherwise.

name: Time Based Key Value Store
description: Design a time-based key-value data structure that can store multiple values for the same key at different timestamps and retrieve the key's value at a certain timestamp.
constraints: All the timestamps `timestamp` of `set` are strictly increasing.
At most `2 * 10^4` calls will be made to `set` and `get`.

name: Split List Largest Sum
description: Given an integer list `nums` and an integer `k`, split `nums` into `k` non-empty contiguous sublists such that the largest sum of any sublist is minimized.

name: Median of Two Sorted Lists
description: Given two sorted lists `nums1` and `nums2` of size `m` and `n` respectively, return the median of the two sorted lists.

name: Find in Mountain List
description: You are given a mountain list `mountain_arr`. You want to find the minimum index `i` such that `mountain_arr.get(i) == target`. You cannot access the mountain list directly. You may only access it using the `MountainList` interface.

name: Excel Sheet Column Title
description: Given an integer `columnNumber`, return its corresponding column title as it appears in an Excel sheet.

name: Greatest Common Divisor of Strings
description: Given two strings `str1` and `str2`, return the largest string `x` such that `x` divides both `str1` and `str2`.
constraints: `str1` and `str2` consist of English uppercase letters.

name: Transpose Matrix
description: Given a 2D integer list `matrix`, return the transpose of `matrix`.

name: Happy Number
description: Determine if a number `n` is happy. A happy number is a number defined by the following process: Starting with any positive integer, replace the number by the sum of the squares of its digits, and repeat the process until the number equals 1, or it loops endlessly in a cycle which does not include 1.

name: Plus One
description: You are given a large integer represented as an integer list `digits`, where each `digits[i]` is the `i`-th digit of the integer. The digits are ordered from most significant to least significant in left-to-right order. The large integer does not contain any leading 0's. Increment the large integer by one and return the resulting list of digits.

name: Roman to Integer
description: Given a roman numeral string `s`, convert it to an integer. The input is guaranteed to be in the range `[1, 3999]`.

name: Pascal's Triangle
description: Given an integer `numRows`, return the first `numRows` of Pascal's triangle.

name: Count Primes
description: Given an integer `n`, return the number of prime numbers that are strictly less than `n`.

name: Insert Greatest Common Divisors in Linked List
description: Given the `head` of a linked list where each node contains an integer value. Between every pair of adjacent nodes, insert a new node with a value equal to the greatest common divisor of the two nodes' values. Return the linked list after insertion.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range `[1, 5000]`.

name: Rotate Image
description: You are given an `n x n` 2D matrix representing an image, rotate the image by 90 degrees (clockwise). You must rotate the image in-place, which means you have to modify the input 2D matrix directly. Return the rotated matrix.

name: Spiral Matrix
description: Given an `m x n` matrix, return all elements of the matrix in spiral order.

name: Set Matrix Zeroes
description: Given an `m x n` integer matrix `matrix`, if an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0's. Return the matrix.

name: Pow(x, n)
description: Implement `pow(x, n)`, which calculates `x` raised to the power `n` (i.e., $x^n$).

name: Multiply Strings
description: Given two non-negative integers `num1` and `num2` represented as strings, return the product of `num1` and `num2`, also represented as a string. Note: You must not convert the inputs to integer directly.
constraints: Both `num1` and `num2` do not contain any leading zero, except the number 0 itself.

name: Detect Squares
description: Detect squares from a stream of 2D points. Implement the `DetectSquares` class to support adding points and counting the number of ways to form axis-aligned squares with a query point.
constraints: At most 3000 calls in total will be made to `add` and `count`.

name: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty list of integers `nums`, every element appears twice except for one. Find that single one.
constraints: Each element in the list appears twice except for one element which appears only once.

name: Number of 1 Bits
description: Write a function that takes an unsigned integer and returns the number of '1' bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).

name: Counting Bits
description: Given an integer `n`, return an list `ans` of length `n + 1` such that for each `i` (`0 <= i <= n`), `ans[i]` is the number of `1` bits in the binary representation of `i`.

name: Add Binary
description: Given two binary strings `a` and `b`, return their sum as a binary string.
constraints: `a` and `b` consist only of '0' or '1' characters.
Each string does not contain leading zeros except for the zero itself.

name: Reverse Bits
description: Reverse bits of a given 32 bits unsigned integer.

name: Missing Number
description: Given an list `nums` containing `n` distinct numbers in the range `[0, n]`, return the only number in the range that is missing from the list.
constraints: All the numbers of `nums` are unique.

name: Sum of Two Integers
description: Given two integers `a` and `b`, return the sum of the two integers without using the operators `+` and `-`.

name: Reverse Integer
description: Given a signed 32-bit integer `x`, return `x` with its digits reversed. If reversing `x` causes the value to go outside the signed 32-bit integer range `[-2^31, 2^31 - 1]`, then return `0`.

name: Bitwise AND of Numbers Range
description: Given two integers `left` and `right` that represent the range `[left, right]`, return the bitwise AND of all numbers in this range, inclusive.

name: Minimum List End
description: Given two integers `n` and `x`, return the minimum possible value of `nums[n - 1]` after constructing a positive integer list `nums` of size `n` with strictly increasing elements such that the bitwise AND of all elements is `x`.

name: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.

name: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge two sorted linked lists into one sorted list.
constraints: Both `list1` and `list2` are sorted in non-decreasing order.

name: Linked List Cycle
description: Given `head`, the head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it.
constraints: `pos` is `-1` or a valid index in the linked-list.

name: Middle of the Linked List
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list, return the middle node of the linked list. If there are two middle nodes, return the second middle node.

name: Design Singly Linked List
description: Design your implementation of the linked list. You can choose to use a singly or doubly linked list.
constraints: At most 2000 calls will be made to `get`, `addAtHead`, `addAtTail`, `addAtIndex`, and `deleteAtIndex`.

name: Reorder List
description: Reorder the singly linked list to be in-place as L0 -> Ln -> L1 -> Ln-1 -> L2 -> Ln-2 -> ...

name: Remove Nth Node From End of List
description: Given the `head` of a linked list, remove the `n`th node from the end of the list and return its head.

name: Copy List With Random Pointer
description: Construct a deep copy of a linked list where each node has a random pointer pointing to any node in the list or null.
constraints: Node.random is null or is pointing to some node in the linked list.

name: Maximum Twin Sum Of A Linked List
description: Return the maximum twin sum of a linked list of even length.

name: Add Two Numbers
description: Add two numbers represented as reverse linked lists and return the sum as a linked list.
constraints: It is guaranteed that the list represents a number that does not have leading zeros.

name: Find The Duplicate Number
description: Find the duplicate number in an list nums containing n + 1 integers where each integer is in the range [1, n] inclusive.
constraints: All the integers in `nums` appear only once except for precisely one integer which appears two or more times.

name: Reverse Linked List II
code: reverseBetween(head: Optional[ListNode], left: int, right: int) -> Optional[ListNode]
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list and two integers `left` and `right` where `left <= right`, reverse the nodes of the list from position `left` to position `right`.

name: Linked List Random Node
description: Given a singly linked list, return a random node's value from the linked list. Each node must have the same probability of being chosen.
constraints: At most 10^4 calls will be made to `getRandom`

name: Design Browser History
description: Design a browser class to visit URLs, and navigate backwards/forwards in history.
constraints: At most 5000 calls will be made to `visit`, `back`, and `forward`.

name: Design Circular Queue
description: Design your implementation of the circular queue.
constraints: At most 3000 calls will be made to `enQueue`, `deQueue`, `Front`, `Rear`, `isEmpty`, and `isFull`.

examples.output: [null, true, true, true, false, 3, true, true, true, 2]

name: Design LRU Cache
description: Design a data structure that follows the constraints of a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache.
constraints: At most 2 * 10^5 calls will be made to `get` and `put`.

name: Design LFU Cache
description: Design and implement a data structure for a Least Frequently Used (LFU) cache.
constraints: At most 2 * 10^5 calls will be made to `get` and `put`.

name: Reverse Nodes In K Group
description: Given the `head` of a linked list, reverse the nodes of the list `k` at a time, and return the modified list.

name: Baseball Game
description: Keep score for a baseball game based on a list of operations.
constraints: `operations[i]` is `'C'`, `'D'`, `'+'`, or an integer in the range `[-3 * 10^4, 3 * 10^4]`

name: Valid Parentheses
description: Determine if an input string containing brackets is valid.

name: Design Stack Using Queues
description: Implement a last-in-first-out (LIFO) stack using queues.
constraints: At most 100 calls will be made to `push`, `pop`, `top`, and `empty`.

name: Design Queue using Stacks
description: Implement a first-in-first-out (FIFO) queue using stacks.
constraints: At most 100 calls will be made to `push`, `pop`, `peek`, and `empty`.

name: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
constraints: At most 3 * 10^4 calls will be made to `push`, `pop`, `top`, and `getMin`.

name: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
description: Evaluate the value of an arithmetic expression in Reverse Polish Notation.
constraints: `tokens[i]` is either an operator, or an integer in the range `[-200, 200]`

name: Asteroid Collision
description: Find the state of the asteroids after all collisions.

name: Daily Temperatures
description: Return an list representing the number of days to wait for a warmer temperature.

name: Online Stock Span
description: Retrieve the span of stock price for the current day.
constraints: At most 10^4 calls will be made to `next`.

name: Car Fleet
code: carFleet(target: int, position: list[int], speed: list[int]) -> int
description: Find the number of car fleets that will arrive at the destination.

name: Simplify Path
description: Simplify an absolute Unix-style path.
constraints: `path` consists of English letters, digits, period `.`, slash `/`, or underscore `_`

name: Decode String
description: Decode an encoded string of the pattern k[encoded_string].
constraints: `s` consists of lowercase English letters, digits, and square brackets `[]`

name: Maximum Frequency Stack
description: Implement a stack-like data structure where the most frequent element is popped first.
constraints: At most 2 * 10^4 calls will be made to `push` and `pop`.

examples.output: [null, null, null, null, null, null, null, 5, 7, 5, 4]

name: Largest Rectangle In Histogram
description: Find the area of the largest rectangle in the histogram.

name: Insert Interval
description: Insert a new interval into a sorted list of non-overlapping intervals and merge if necessary.

name: Merge Intervals
description: Merge all overlapping intervals.

name: Non Overlapping Intervals
description: Find the minimum number of intervals to remove to make the rest non-overlapping.

name: Meeting Rooms
description: Determine if a person could attend all meetings.

name: Meeting Rooms II
description: Find the minimum number of conference rooms required.

name: Meeting Rooms III
description: Return the number of the room that held the most meetings.

name: Minimum Interval to Include Each Query
description: For each query, find the size of the smallest interval that contains it.

name: Lemonade Change
description: Determine if you can provide correct change to every customer in a line.

name: Maximum Sublist
description: Find the contiguous sublist which has the largest sum and return its sum.

name: Maximum Sum Circular Sublist
description: Find the maximum possible sum of a non-empty sublist of a circular list.

name: Longest Turbulent Sublist
description: Return the length of a maximum size turbulent sublist of arr.

name: Jump Game
description: Determine if you are able to reach the last index starting from the first index.

name: Jump Game II
description: Return the minimum number of jumps to reach the last index.

name: Jump Game VII
code: canReach(s: str, minJump: int, maxJump: int) -> bool
description: Determine if you can reach the last index of binary string s.

name: Gas Station
description: Find the starting gas station's index if you can travel around the circuit once.

name: Hand of Straights
description: Determine if a hand of cards can be rearranged into groups of groupSize consecutive cards.

name: Dota2 Senate
description: Predict which party victory is guaranteed based on the Dota2 Senate.

name: Merge Triplets to Form Target Triplet
description: Determine if you can obtain target by merging triplets.

name: Partition Labels
description: Partition string s into as many parts as possible so that each letter appears in at most one part.

name: Valid Parenthesis String
description: Determine if s is a valid parenthesis string considering asterisks.

name: Candy
description: Find the minimum number of candies you must give to the children.

name: Design Elevator System
description: Design an elevator system using the SCAN scheduling algorithm.

name: Binary Tree Inorder Traversal
description: Return the inorder traversal of a binary tree's nodes' values.

name: Binary Tree Preorder Traversal
description: Return the preorder traversal of a binary tree's nodes' values.

name: Binary Tree Postorder Traversal
description: Return the postorder traversal of a binary tree's nodes' values.

name: Invert Binary Tree
description: Invert a binary tree (mirror it) and return its root.

name: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Find the maximum depth (number of nodes along the longest path) of a binary tree.

name: Diameter of Binary Tree
description: Return the length of the diameter (longest path between any two nodes) of the tree.

name: Balanced Binary Tree
description: Determine if a binary tree is height-balanced (depth of the two subtrees of every node never differs by more than 1).

name: Same Tree
description: Check if two binary trees are structurally identical and have the same node values.

name: Subtree of Another Tree
description: Check if a binary tree `subRoot` is structurally identical to a subtree of `root`.

examples.input: root = T[3, 4, 5, 1, 2, null, null, null, null, 0], subRoot = T[4, 1, 2]

name: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree
code: lowestCommonAncestor(root: TreeNode, p: TreeNode, q: TreeNode) -> TreeNode
description: Find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) node of two given nodes in a BST.

name: Insert into a Binary Search Tree
description: Insert a value into a BST and return the root of the modified BST.

examples.input: root = T[4, 2, 7, 1, 3, null, null, null, null, null, null], val = 5

name: Delete Node in a BST
description: Delete a node with a given key from a BST and return the root.

name: Design Binary Tree
description: Design a binary tree class that supports complete level-order insertion and traversals.

name: Binary Search Tree Iterator
description: Implement an iterator over the in-order traversal of a BST.

name: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
description: Return the level order traversal of its nodes' values (i.e., from left to right, level by level).

name: Binary Tree Right Side View
description: Return the values of the nodes you can see ordered from top to bottom when standing on the right side of the tree.

name: Count Good Nodes In Binary Tree
description: Count the number of 'good' nodes (nodes where in the path from root to that node there are no nodes with a value greater than it).

name: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Determine if a binary tree is a valid Binary Search Tree (BST).

name: Kth Smallest Element In a Bst
description: Find the `k`th smallest element (1-indexed) in a BST.

name: Construct Binary Tree From Preorder And Inorder Traversal
description: Construct and return the binary tree from its preorder and inorder traversals.

name: House Robber III
description: Find the maximum amount of money you can rob from binary tree nodes without robbing two directly-linked parent-child nodes.

name: Delete Leaves With a Given Value
description: Delete all leaf nodes with a value equal to `target` (and if deleting them produces new leaves of that value, delete those too).

name: Range Sum Query - Mutable
description: Implement an list class that supports single-element updates and range sum queries dynamically.

name: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
description: Find the maximum path sum of any non-empty path in a binary tree.

name: Serialize And Deserialize Binary Tree
description: Design an algorithm to serialize and deserialize a binary tree.

name: Count of Smaller Numbers After Self
description: Given an integer list `nums`, return an integer list `counts` where `counts[i]` is the number of smaller elements to the right of `nums[i]`.

name: AVL Tree
description: Design a self-balancing binary search tree (AVL tree) supporting insert, delete, and height maintenance.

name: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
description: Implement a trie (prefix tree) with `insert`, `search`, and `startsWith` methods.
constraints: `word` and `prefix` consist only of lowercase English letters.

examples.output: [null, null, true, false, true, null, true]

name: Design Add And Search Words Data Structure
description: Design a data structure that supports adding new words and finding if a string matches any previously added string, including support for the dot character `.` to represent any single letter.
constraints: `word` in `addWord` consists of lowercase English letters.
`word` in `search` consists of lowercase English letters or `.`

examples.output: [null, null, null, null, false, true, true, true]

name: Extra Characters in a String
description: Given a string `s` and a dictionary of words `dictionary`, break `s` into one or more non-overlapping substrings present in `dictionary`. Return the minimum number of extra characters left over.
constraints: `s` and `dictionary[i]` consist of lowercase English letters.

name: Word Search II
description: Given an `m x n` board of characters and a list of strings `words`, return all words on the board.

name: Design In-Memory File System
description: Design an in-memory file system to simulate the behaviors: `ls`, `mkdir`, `addContentToFile`, and `readContentFromFile`.
constraints: `path` and `filePath` consist of lowercase letters and `/`.

name: Kth Largest Element in a Stream
description: Design a class to find the `k`-th largest element in a stream. Note that it is the `k`-th largest element in the sorted order, not the `k`-th distinct element.
constraints: At most 10^4 calls will be made to add.

name: Last Stone Weight
description: You are given a list of integers `stones` where `stones[i]` is the weight of the `i`-th stone. We are playing a game with the stones. On each turn, we choose the heaviest two stones and smash them together. Return the weight of the last remaining stone. If there are no stones left, return `0`.

name: K Closest Points to Origin
description: Given a list of `points` where `points[i] = [xi, yi]` represents a point on the **X-Y** plane and an integer `k`, return the `k` closest points to the origin `(0, 0)`. Return the list of points sorted by distance to the origin in ascending order. If two points have the same distance, sort them by their X-coordinate, then Y-coordinate.

name: Kth Largest Element in a List
description: Given an integer list `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k`-th largest element in the list. Note that it is the `k`-th largest element in the sorted order, not the `k`-th distinct element.

name: Task Scheduler
description: Given a characters list `tasks`, representing the tasks a CPU needs to do, where each character represents a different task. Return the minimum number of units of time that the CPU will take to finish all the given tasks with a cooldown period `n` between two same tasks.
constraints: tasks[i] is an uppercase English letter.

name: Design Heap
description: Design a min-heap data structure from scratch. A min-heap is a binary tree where the value of each node is greater than or equal to the value of its parent.

name: Design Heap Sort
description: Given a list of integers `nums`, sort the list in ascending order using the heap sort algorithm. Implement this without using Python's built-in sorting or heapq modules.

name: Design Twitter
description: Design a simplified version of Twitter where users can post tweets, follow/unfollow other users, and are able to see the 10 most recent tweets in the user's news feed.
constraints: At most 10^4 calls will be made to Twitter operations.

name: Single Threaded CPU
description: Implement single-threaded CPU processing order. If CPU is idle, it picks the task with the shortest processing time (with index tie-breaker).

name: Reorganize String
description: Given a string `s`, rearrange the characters of `s` so that any two adjacent characters are not the same. If not possible, return `""`. Tie-breaker: choose frequency then lexicographical smaller character.
constraints: s consists of lowercase English letters.

name: Longest Happy String
code: longestDiverseString(a: int, b: int, c: int) -> str
description: Given three integers `a`, `b`, and `c`, return the longest possible happy string containing at most `a` 'a's, `b` 'b's, and `c` 'c's, with no three consecutive same characters.

name: Car Pooling
description: Determine if it is possible to carry all passengers for all trips without exceeding the capacity of the car.

name: Merge K Sorted Lists
description: Merge `k` sorted linked lists into one sorted linked list.
constraints: The sum of lists[i].length will not exceed 10^4.

name: Find Median from Data Stream
description: Implement a class to find the median of a stream of integers.
constraints: `At most 5 * 10^4 calls will be made to addNum and findMedian.`

name: IPO
code: findMaximizedCapital(k: int, w: int, profits: list[int], capital: list[int]) -> int
description: Find the maximum capital that can be obtained after finishing at most `k` distinct projects.

name: Path Sum
description: Given the `root` of a binary tree and an integer `targetSum`, return `true` if the tree has a root-to-leaf path such that adding up all the values along the path equals `targetSum`.
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 5000].

examples.input: root = T[5, 4, 8, 11, None, 13, 4, 7, 2, None, None, None, 1], targetSum = 22

name: Sum of All Subsets XOR Total
description: The **XOR sum** of an array is the XOR sum of all its elements, or `0` if the array is empty. Return the sum of all **XOR sums** for every subset of `nums`.

name: Subsets
description: Given an integer list `nums` of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set). The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets. Return the solution in any order.
constraints: All the numbers of nums are unique.

name: Combination Sum
description: Given a list of **distinct** integers `candidates` and a target integer `target`, return a list of all unique combinations of `candidates` where the chosen numbers sum to `target`. You may return the combinations in any order. The same number may be chosen from `candidates` an unlimited number of times.
constraints: All elements of candidates are distinct.

name: Combination Sum II
description: Given a collection of candidate numbers `candidates` and a target number `target`, find all unique combinations in `candidates` where the candidate numbers sum to `target`. Each number in `candidates` may only be used once in the combination.

name: Combinations
description: Given two integers `n` and `k`, return all possible combinations of `k` numbers chosen from the range `[1, n]`.

name: Permutations
description: Given a list `nums` of distinct integers, return all the possible permutations. You can return the answer in any order.
constraints: All the integers of nums are unique.

name: Subsets II
description: Given an integer list `nums` that may contain duplicates, return all possible subsets. The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets.

name: Permutations II
description: Given a collection of numbers `nums` that might contain duplicates, return all unique permutations in any order.

name: Generate Parentheses
description: Given `n` pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.

name: Word Search
description: Given an `m x n` grid of characters `board` and a string `word`, return `true` if `word` exists in the grid. The word can be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells.
constraints: `board and word consist of lowercase/uppercase letters.`

name: Palindrome Partitioning
description: Given a string `s`, partition `s` such that every substring of the partition is a palindrome. Return all possible palindrome partitionings of `s`.
constraints: s contains only lowercase English letters.

name: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
description: Given a string containing digits from `2-9` inclusive, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent.
constraints: digits[i] is a digit in the range ['2', '9'].

name: Matchsticks to Square
description: Determine if you can make a square by linking up all matchsticks without breaking them. Each matchstick must be used exactly once.

name: Partition to K Equal Sum Subsets
description: Return `true` if it is possible to divide the list `nums` into `k` non-empty subsets whose sums are all equal.
constraints: The sum of nums[i] is at most 10^5.

name: N Queens
description: Return all distinct board configurations of placement of queens on an `n x n` chessboard.

name: N Queens II
description: Return the number of distinct solutions to the **n-queens puzzle** on an `n x n` chessboard.

name: Word Break II
description: Given a string `s` and a dictionary of strings `wordDict`, add spaces in `s` to construct a sentence where each word is a valid dictionary word. Return all such possible sentences.
constraints: s and wordDict[i] consist of lowercase English letters.
All the strings of wordDict are unique.
Input is guaranteed to be generateable from dict.

examples.output: ["pine apple pen apple", "pine applepen apple", "pineapple pen apple"]

name: Island Perimeter
description: You are given `grid` an `row x col` grid representing a map where `1` represents land and `0` represents water. Grid cells are connected horizontally/vertically (not diagonally). The grid is completely surrounded by water, and there is exactly one island (i.e., one or more connected land cells). Determine the perimeter of the island.
constraints: There is exactly one island in the grid.

name: Flood Fill
code: floodFill(image: list[list[int]], sr: int, sc: int, color: int) -> list[list[int]]
description: An image is represented by an `m x n` integer grid `image` where `image[i][j]` represents the pixel value of the image. You are also given three integers `sr`, `sc`, and `color`. Perform a flood fill on the image starting from the pixel `image[sr][sc]`.

name: Verifying An Alien Dictionary
description: In an alien language, surprisingly, they also use English lowercase letters, but possibly in a different `order`. Given a list of `words` written in the alien language, and the `order` of the alphabet, return `true` if and only if the given `words` are sorted lexicographically in this alien language.
constraints: All characters in words and order are lowercase English letters.
All characters in order are unique.

name: Find the Town Judge
description: In a town, there are `n` people labeled from `1` to `n`. There is a rumor that one of these people is secretly the town judge. If the town judge exists, return the label of the town judge. Otherwise, return `-1`.
constraints: All the trust pairs are unique.

name: Matrix Depth-First Search
description: Given an `m x n` binary grid `grid`, return the path of a Depth-First Search traversal starting from cell `(0, 0)`. When exploring adjacent cells (up, down, left, right), traverse in the order: Right, Down, Left, Up. Cells with value `1` are blocked and cannot be traversed. Return the values of 1D coordinate representation (i.e. `r * n + c`) of the path. A cell can only be visited once.

name: Matrix Breadth-First Search
description: Given an `m x n` binary grid `grid`, return the path of a Breadth-First Search traversal starting from cell `(0, 0)`. When exploring adjacent cells (up, down, left, right), traverse in the order: Right, Down, Left, Up. Cells with value `1` are blocked and cannot be traversed. Return the values of 1D coordinate representation (i.e. `r * n + c`) of the path. A cell can only be visited once.

name: Number of Islands
description: Given an `m x n` 2D binary grid `grid` which represents a map of `'1'`s (land) and `'0'`s (water), return the number of islands.

name: Max Area of Island
description: Given a binary matrix `grid` of size `m x n`, return the maximum area of an island in `grid`. If there is no island, return `0`.

name: Design Graph
description: Design a directed weighted graph class `Graph` that supports finding the shortest path between two nodes using Dijkstra's algorithm.
constraints: At most 100 calls will be made to addEdge and shortestPath.

name: Clone Graph
description: Given an adjacency list `adjList` representing a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy (clone) of the graph as a new adjacency list. The input `adjList` is 1-indexed, meaning `adjList[i]` contains the list of neighbors of the `(i+1)`-th node.
constraints: The graph is connected and has no duplicate edges or self-loops.

name: Walls And Gates
description: You are given an `m x n` grid `rooms` initialized with three possible values: `-1` (a wall or obstacle), `0` (a gate), or `2147483647` (an empty room). Fill each empty room with the distance to its nearest gate. If it is impossible to reach a gate, it should be filled with `2147483647`.

name: Shortest Path in Binary Matrix
description: Given an `n x n` binary matrix `grid`, return the length of the shortest clear path in the matrix. If there is no clear path, return `-1`. A clear path starts from `(0, 0)` and ends at `(n-1, n-1)` traversing 8-directionally.

name: Rotting Oranges
description: Given an `m x n` grid where each cell can have value `0` (empty cell), `1` (fresh orange), or `2` (rotten orange). Return the minimum number of minutes that must elapse until no cell has a fresh orange. If this is impossible, return `-1`.

name: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
description: Given an `m x n` grid of integers `heights` representing the height above sea level of each unit cell, return a list of grid coordinates `[r, c]` where water can flow to both the Pacific and Atlantic oceans.

name: Surrounded Regions
description: Given an `m x n` matrix `board` containing `'X'` and `'O'`, capture all regions that are 4-directionally surrounded by `'X'`. A region is captured by flipping all `'O'`s into `'X'`s in that surrounded region.

name: Open The Lock
description: You have a lock with 4 circular wheels, initially set to `'0000'`. You are given a list of `deadends` keys and a `target` key. Return the minimum total number of turns required to open the lock, or `-1` if it is impossible.
constraints: target and deadends consist of digits only.

name: Course Schedule
description: There are a total of `numCourses` courses you have to take, labeled from `0` to `numCourses - 1`. You are given a list `prerequisites` where `prerequisites[i] = [ai, bi]` indicates that you must take course `bi` first if you want to take course `ai`. Determine if you can finish all courses.

name: Course Schedule II
description: Return the ordering of courses you should take to finish all courses. If there are multiple valid orderings, return the one that is lexicographically smallest (choose the smallest course ID first at each split point). If it is impossible to finish all courses, return an empty list.

name: Graph Valid Tree
description: Given `n` nodes labeled from `0` to `n - 1` and a list of undirected edges, write a function to check whether these edges make up a valid tree.

name: Course Schedule IV
code: checkIfPrerequisite(numCourses: int, prerequisites: list[list[int]], queries: list[list[int]]) -> list[bool]
description: There are a total of `numCourses` courses. You are given prerequisites and queries. For each query `[u, v]`, return if `u` is a prerequisite of `v`.

name: Number of Connected Components In An Undirected Graph
description: You have a graph of `n` nodes. You are given an integer `n` and an array `edges`. Return the number of connected components in the graph.

name: Redundant Connection
description: Return an edge that can be removed so that the resulting graph is a tree of `n` nodes. If there are multiple answers, return the answer that occurs last in the input.

name: Accounts Merge
description: Given a list of `accounts` where each element `accounts[i]` is a list of strings, merge the accounts with common emails. Two accounts belong to the same person if there is some common email. The output should be sorted: each merged account should have its emails sorted alphabetically, and the accounts list itself should be sorted lexicographically.
constraints: accounts[i][j] (for j > 0) is a valid email address.

name: Evaluate Division
code: calcEquation(equations: list[list[str]], values: list[float], queries: list[list[str]]) -> list[float]
description: You are given equations and values, and a set of queries. Compute the value of each query. If the value is undefined, return `-1.0`.

name: Minimum Height Trees
description: A tree is an undirected graph in which any two vertices are connected by exactly one path. Find all Minimum Height Trees (MHTs) and return a list of their root labels in sorted order.
constraints: The input graph is guaranteed to be a tree.

name: Word Ladder
code: ladderLength(beginWord: str, endWord: str, wordList: list[str]) -> int
description: Given two words, `beginWord` and `endWord`, and a dictionary `wordList`, return the number of words in the shortest transformation sequence from `beginWord` to `endWord`, or `0` if no such sequence exists.
constraints: All words consist of lowercase English letters.
All words in wordList are unique.
